data.title: Equity Bancshares Earnings Call Highlights Growth Momentum
data.description:
Equity Bancshares Inc. ((EQBK)) has held its Q1 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Equity Bancshares’ latest earnings call struck an upbeat tone, with management emphasizing strong momentum from recent acquisitions, record revenue, and solid profitability. While integration costs, funding pressures, and a temporary uptick in credit issues weighed on some metrics, executives argued that scale benefits, cost savings, and a healthy capital base position the bank well for the next phase of growth.

Acquisitions and Asset Growth

The Frontier acquisition was the star of the quarter, driving roughly 20% asset growth and helping deliver record quarterly revenue. Combined with another recent deal, Equity Bancshares’ balance sheet is now more than 40% larger than it was a year ago, underscoring the company’s strategy of using M&A to build regional scale.

Adjusted Earnings and EPS Improvement

GAAP net income came in at $17.0 million, or $0.80 per diluted share, but adjusted results painted a stronger picture as noncore items were stripped out. Adjusted net income rose to $26.2 million, or $1.23 per share, up from $23.3 million and $1.21 in the prior quarter, with adjusted earnings on tangible common equity climbing to $27.7 million.

Strong Return Metrics

Profitability remained a key highlight, with adjusted return on average tangible common equity reaching 16.1% for the quarter. That level of return signals that the enlarged franchise is generating attractive earnings on shareholder capital, even as integration work and higher funding costs flow through the income statement.

Net Interest Income and Margin Outlook

Net interest income rose sharply to $73.7 million, an increase of $10.2 million from the prior quarter as new assets and higher loan yields kicked in. The reported net interest margin was 4.33%, or 4.29% on a normalized basis, and management guided to a full-year margin range of 4.20% to 4.35% as funding is repriced and acquisition effects normalize.

Loan Production and Pipeline

Loan production was robust at $267 million, up 21.7% from the prior quarter, with new originations carrying an average rate of 6.87%, about 10 basis points higher. The bank’s 75% probability loan pipeline stood at a sizable $517 million, and management signaled expectations for mid-single-digit organic loan growth on top of acquisition-fueled expansion.

Deposit Growth and Funding Trends

Total deposits surged by approximately $1.2 billion in the quarter, reflecting both acquired balances and healthy growth across legacy markets. Noninterest-bearing deposits represented 20.2% of the total, and several community markets recorded more than 5% quarterly growth, with North Central Missouri posting about 7% growth, supporting future margin stability.

Capital Strength and Share Repurchases

Capital levels remained solid despite the larger balance sheet, with tangible common equity at 9%, CET1 at 11.5%, and total capital at 14.4%. The company also returned capital to shareholders through the repurchase of 500,000 shares at an average price of $44.74, while maintaining capacity for additional buybacks and future M&A activity.

Integration and Cost-Save Progress

Management reported smooth progress on integrating Frontier, noting that the core system conversion was completed on schedule. Technology and personnel-related cost savings are already emerging, and the bank expects to reach the full run-rate in the second quarter, tracking at or slightly ahead of a conservative roughly 23% cost-save target for the deal.

Margin Compression from Acquisition Funding Mix

Even with higher net interest income, the funding profile of the acquired Frontier franchise caused some net interest margin compression, with NIM slipping from 4.47% to 4.33% quarter over quarter. Purchase accounting accretion added about 19 basis points, or roughly $3.3 million, but management expects this to normalize to around $2.5 million in the coming quarters, creating some periodic margin volatility.

Integration-Related Expense Increase

Noninterest expense totaled $55.0 million in the quarter, but on an adjusted basis, excluding merger-related items, it was $49.2 million versus $44.1 million previously. That 11.5% linked-quarter increase was largely tied to integration and M&A work, yet the adjusted expense-to-assets ratio still improved by 25 basis points to 2.57%, showing emerging efficiency gains.

Credit Metrics Impacted by Acquisition

Credit quality metrics moved higher, though management stressed that the deterioration was mainly tied to acquired portfolios and technical issues rather than broad-based weakness. Nonperforming assets rose to $58.3 million and nonaccrual loans to $52.4 million, pushing the NPA ratio to 0.8%, but roughly $30 million of problem balances stem from short-term renewal process issues that are expected to be resolved in the second quarter.

Provision and Reserve Dynamics

The quarter’s results included a $6 million loan loss provision mainly related to acquired loans, reflecting conservative treatment of the new portfolios. The allowance for credit losses stood at 1.18%, or 1.77% including merger discounts, and management suggested that reserves may gradually trend back toward about 1.23% as purchase accounting marks run off over time.

Reliance on Purchase Accounting Accretion

Purchase accounting accretion was a meaningful tailwind to earnings and margins, with loan accretion contributing roughly $3.3 million this quarter. As that support normalizes toward about $2.5 million, investors should expect some moderation in accretion-driven income, making underlying loan yields, funding costs, and cost controls increasingly important drivers of reported results.

Short-Term Funding Complexity

The Frontier deal also brought a mix of brokered and other higher-cost funding that the bank is actively working to reprice or replace. Management highlighted that about $100 million of this funding was repriced in the quarter, moving from roughly 4.50% to nearer 3.75%, but acknowledged that transitional funding cost pressure will remain a near-term headwind as this process continues.

Guidance and Forward-Looking Outlook

Looking ahead, management reaffirmed its full-year net interest margin outlook of 4.20% to 4.35% and expressed confidence in achieving a $5.00 per-share earnings target in 2026. The team guided to mid-single-digit organic loan growth, an effective tax rate of 22% to 23%, a normalized quarterly provision in the $1.5 million to $2.0 million range, and continued strong capital generation of more than $25 million per quarter, supporting both growth and buybacks.

Equity Bancshares’ call underscored a franchise in transition but moving in a clearly positive direction, as recent acquisitions scale up earnings power despite temporary integration and funding friction. With healthy returns, solid capital, an active loan pipeline, and a clear plan to optimize funding and costs, management presented a constructive medium-term story that may appeal to investors seeking regional bank growth with disciplined risk management.

data.title: SurgePays Earnings Call Balances Growth With Liquidity Risk
data.description:
Surgepays Inc ((SURG)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

SurgePays’ latest earnings call struck a cautious but constructive tone, blending evidence of operational progress with stark reminders of funding and transition risk after the loss of key subsidies. Management highlighted strong scalability, tighter cost controls, and healthier unit economics, but investors were also warned about declining annual revenue, thin cash reserves, and continued dependence on external capital.

Demonstrated scalability in 2025

Revenue progression through 2025 showcased how quickly SurgePays can scale when capital is deployed, with sales rising from $10.6M in Q1 to $11.5M in Q2 and then jumping to $18.7M in Q3, a 62.6% sequential surge. Executives framed this pattern as proof that the model can accelerate rapidly once marketing and subscriber acquisition spending is turned back on.

Meaningful cost reductions

Management underscored aggressive cost discipline, with total G&A falling to about $20.1M from $27.5M, a roughly 26.9% reduction year over year. SG&A excluding depreciation and amortization dropped similarly to around $19.2M from $26.3M, reflecting cuts in compensation, professional services, and contractor spending aimed at creating a leaner base.

Improved gross and operating losses

Operational efficiency gains translated into narrower losses, as gross loss improved to roughly $10.6M from $14.3M, a 25.9% improvement versus the prior year. Net loss from operations also moved in the right direction, shrinking to about $30.7M from $41.8M, or roughly 26.6% better, signaling progress toward a more sustainable business model.

Strong growth in point-of-sale and prepaid services

Investors were told that the Point-of-sale and Prepaid Services segment expanded by about $26.1M year over year, becoming a key growth driver. This higher-margin business partially offset the decline in subsidized and MVNO revenue and is central to management’s effort to shift the company’s mix toward more profitable recurring streams.

Diversified revenue streams and distribution footprint

SurgePays emphasized that it is no longer tied to a single subsidized program, now operating across subsidized wireless, LinkUp Mobile prepaid, wholesale MVNE deals, and POS fintech and data services. The company’s retail distribution network now spans more than 9,000 locations, while its online platform serves as a customer acquisition engine for these multiple revenue channels.

Reduced current monthly cash burn

To address investor concern over liquidity, management said actions taken since year-end have lowered the estimated monthly cash burn to roughly $250K–$300K by the end of Q1 2026. These reductions are driven by ongoing cost controls and efficiency moves, with executives arguing that the trimmed expense base gives them more time to execute the transition plan.

Full-year revenue decline

Despite operational improvements, total revenue for 2025 slipped to about $57.0M from $60.9M, a 6.4% drop year over year that management tied mainly to the expected falloff in subsidized revenue following the expiration of the Affordable Connectivity Program. The decline underscores the scale of the transition the company must navigate as it pivots away from legacy subsidy-driven volumes.

Q4 sequential revenue pullback

Fourth-quarter revenue came in at $16.2M, down about 13.4% from the $18.7M generated in Q3, reflecting a deliberate pullback in subscriber acquisition spending after the earlier ramp. While Q4 was still stronger than the same quarter a year earlier, management acknowledged that the retreat from the Q3 inflection point highlighted the sensitivity of growth to capital deployment.

Low year-end cash and working capital deterioration

The balance sheet remained a central concern, with year-end cash of roughly $1.7M and working capital flipping to a deficit of about $16.2M versus an $11.8M surplus the year before, a roughly $28.0M negative swing. Management conceded that this deterioration signals meaningful liquidity pressure and makes continued funding and execution discipline critical in the coming quarters.

Significant operating cash usage and reliance on financing

Net cash used in operating activities totaled about $21.3M for 2025, illustrating how cash-hungry the current model remains despite cost cuts and better margins. To bridge the gap, SurgePays raised approximately $10.5M from financing activities, including use of its ATM facility, highlighting ongoing dependence on external capital to support operations and future growth.

Nonrecurring Q4 costs and legal expenses

The company noted that fourth-quarter SG&A included around $2.3M of nonrecurring or noncash items, including legal expenses, which management said distorted the quarter’s profitability picture. Executives stressed that these charges are not representative of the ongoing run rate and that normalized expenses should be lower going forward.

Ongoing revenue transition and execution risk

Management acknowledged that the business remains in transition after the subsidy program’s expiration, with plans to redeploy capital into LinkUp Mobile and other channels when funding allows. However, sequential revenue volatility and the sizable working capital deficit underscore that execution, access to capital, and timing of growth investments remain key risks for shareholders.

Forward outlook and operational focus

While avoiding formal numerical guidance, SurgePays laid out priorities centered on improving gross margins, maintaining strict cost discipline, and selectively deploying capital across multiple channels, including prepaid, subsidized wireless, wholesale MVNE, and POS fintech. Management believes the slimmer expense base and diversified platform can support scalable growth over time, but emphasized that 2026 will be judged on tangible progress, not promises.

Investors listening to SurgePays’ call heard a company advancing on efficiency and diversification while still wrestling with shrinking legacy revenue and thin liquidity. The message was that the model can scale and losses are narrowing, yet successful execution and stable financing will determine whether recent gains translate into durable value for shareholders.

data.title: Cota Confirms Ransomware Behind System Disruption, Operations Unaffected
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Cota Co., Ltd. ( (JP:4923) ).

Cota Co., Ltd. has confirmed that the system disruption it disclosed on March 30, 2026, was caused by a ransomware attack, following an investigation conducted with external cybersecurity experts. The company has isolated potentially infected internal systems and disconnected its network as a precaution, while withholding technical details of the ransomware to prevent further damage.

The company reports that, as of now, there is no confirmed leakage of personal or other sensitive information, and it pledges to promptly disclose any data breach should it be identified. Operationally, Cota states that production, order receipt, and shipping remain unaffected and continue as normal, suggesting limited short-term impact on business continuity but highlighting ongoing cybersecurity risk management for stakeholders.

More about Cota Co., Ltd.

Cota Co., Ltd. is a Japan-based company listed on the TSE Prime Market under securities code 4923, operating in the cosmetics and beauty-care industry. Headquartered in Kumiyama-cho, Kuse-gun, Kyoto Prefecture, it focuses on manufacturing and supplying haircare and related cosmetic products, primarily serving professional salons and beauty-related business partners across its domestic market.

Average Trading Volume: 55,152

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: Yen33.63B

data.title: SPC Global strengthens board with appointment of Rob Perry to lead Audit & Risk
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Food Revolution Group Ltd. ( (AU:SPG) ) has provided an announcement.

SPC Global Holdings has appointed Rob Perry as an independent non-executive director and chair of its Audit & Risk Committee, effective 1 May 2026. The appointment brings the board a seasoned governance specialist with deep experience in financial oversight for complex listed companies.

Perry spent more than three decades in audit, risk management and governance at EY, including senior regional leadership roles in risk consulting and governance services. He currently serves on the boards and audit committees of major Australian corporates and public bodies, reinforcing his credentials in high-level financial stewardship.

SPC Global chair Andrew Reitzer said Perry’s appointment will strengthen the board’s ability to oversee risk, financial performance and disciplined balance sheet management as the group executes its strategy. The move underscores SPC Global’s focus on robust governance as it pursues growth in its food and beverage portfolio across Australia, Asia and international markets.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:SPG) stock is a Buy with a A$0.75 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Food Revolution Group Ltd. stock, see the AU:SPG Stock Forecast page.

More about Food Revolution Group Ltd.

SPC Global Holdings Ltd is a food and beverage group listed on the ASX that brings together SPC, The Original Beverage Co, Nature One and Natural Ingredients. The company focuses on nourishment and wellness products including packaged fruit and tomatoes, baked beans and spaghetti, ready-made meals, beverages, juice and wellness shots, powdered milk, and fruit and vegetable supplies for the food service industry.

With around 800 employees and operations across Australia and Asia, SPC Global targets both domestic and international growth in consumer and food service markets. Its diversified portfolio positions the group to tap rising demand for convenient, health-oriented products in retail and food service channels.

data.title: King Global Ventures Hires Marketing Firms to Boost Investor Awareness
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from King Global Ventures ( (TSE:KING) ).

King Global Ventures has appointed Redfern Media, Cernivard Group and Florida-based consultant Rob Schneider as marketing consultants to expand investor awareness through social media, roadshows, investor relations support and broader media exposure. The moves signal a concerted push to raise the company’s capital markets profile and attract new shareholders as it advances its copper and gold exploration projects in Arizona and Ontario.

Under the new agreements, Redfern is retained for at least three months at $5,000 per month plus restricted share units and options, while Cernivard will receive a six-month monthly retainer of US$7,500 for investor relations services. Schneider has been engaged for six months for US$92,500, payable in restricted share units, aligning compensation with equity performance and underscoring King Global’s strategy of using targeted marketing to support its exploration-focused growth plans.

More about King Global Ventures

King Global Ventures Inc. is a Vancouver-based mineral exploration company focused on precious and base metals projects in North America. Its portfolio includes the early-stage Black Canyon copper-gold-silver-zinc VMS project in Yavapai County, Arizona, which encompasses 213 concessions and several past-producing mines, as well as the Mikwam gold property in Ontario’s Abitibi Greenstone Belt, which hosts historical inferred gold resources.

data.title: Elevate Service Group Builds National Platform With Think Green and JJ&A Deals
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Elevate Service Group Inc ( (TSE:SERV) ).

Elevate Service Group has completed its acquisition of Think Green Solutions, a national provider of turnkey energy-efficient LED lighting and control solutions for retrofit and new construction projects. The deal establishes a scalable national lighting and energy optimization segment for Elevate and is expected to be immediately accretive to earnings, while Think Green’s founders will remain in leadership roles to drive further growth.

The company also acquired JJ&A Mechanical and confirmed the full-time appointment of Frank Guo as chief financial officer, underscoring its strategy to create a leading national facilities services platform. Together, these moves deepen Elevate’s capabilities in lighting and mechanical services, support margin expansion, and strengthen its competitive position serving multi-site commercial and industrial customers in Canada and the United States.

More about Elevate Service Group Inc

Elevate Service Group Inc. is a Toronto-based facilities services company focused on building a vertically integrated, national platform. Through targeted acquisitions, it offers a range of services including energy-efficient LED lighting, controls, and mechanical solutions to multi-site commercial and industrial clients across Canada, with a growing U.S. presence.
